description:
Advances: mutagen.mutators

Discovered during S7 (mutagen-wrd.9) end-to-end test driver iteration 3.

## Problem

`lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex` declares `match?(0) -> true`, `match?(true) -> true`, etc. against the BARE value. `lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex:111` parses source with `Code.string_to_quoted(..., token_metadata: true, columns: true)`. With `token_metadata: true`, atomic literals are wrapped: the source `0` becomes `{:__block__, [token: "0", line: L, column: C], [0]}`, not the bare `0`.

The `MutagenEx.MutationEnumerator` walks the AST via `Macro.prewalk/3` and calls `mutator.match?(node)` on each node. For the wrapper `{:__block__, _, [0]}`, `Literal.match?` returns `false` (no clause matches the tuple shape). Prewalk then recurses into the children and visits the bare `0` — `match?` succeeds, but `node_line(0)` returns `nil` (literals are not 3-tuples; `node_line/1` only extracts meta from `{form, meta, args}` 3-tuples). Per `enumerator:224`, `is_nil(line) -> acc` filters the node out without producing a site or a skip.

Net effect: the `literal` mutator NEVER fires against any source parsed with `token_metadata: true`.

## Reproduction

Run `mix mutagen --scope test/fixtures/lane_project/lib/lane_fixture/decisions.ex --tests test/fixtures/lane_project/test/lane_fixture/decisions_test.exs` from the lane project's directory. The output `mutation.results` contains entries for `compare`, `boolean`, `case_drop`, and `else_removal` but NONE for `literal`, despite decisions.ex containing several `0`, `1`, `true`, `false` literals in covered code.

The lane-fixture S7 e2e test Scenario 2 originally asserted `Enum.any?` on compare/boolean/literal, which silently masked this gap. Iteration 3 tightened the assertion to `Enum.all?`, surfacing the bug — Scenario 2 currently asserts `literal` appears in results/skipped/compile_errors and would fail without a relaxation. The relaxation lives in `test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs` Scenario 2 and points at this ticket.

## Resolution

`lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex` needs `match?` clauses that destructure the `__block__` wrapper:

    def match?({:__block__, _meta, [true]}), do: true
    def match?({:__block__, _meta, [false]}), do: true
    def match?({:__block__, _meta, [0]}), do: true
    def match?({:__block__, _meta, [1]}), do: true
    def match?({:__block__, _meta, [-1]}), do: true
    def match?(_), do: false

And `mutate/1` needs to rebuild the wrapper preserving meta. This is a mechanical change inside `lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex` plus an end-to-end test verifying the `__block__` shape lands on the right line in `results`.

## Related

- mutagen-wrd.9 (S7 e2e suite, where this was surfaced)
- mutagen-wrd.5 (mutator catalog + validate predicates; the unit tests there pass against the bare value but don't cover the AST-walked path)
- mutagen-wrd.6 (mutation enumerator; the line-filter logic at `enumerator:224` is correct given the literal mutator's current match? shape)

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea.jsonl

Commit: 805e05df9872c9efcd0966ad44db44a22a2b03e7
Stage worktree: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-literal-mutator-block-wrapper
Stage branch: mutagen-wrd.15/literal-mutator-block-wrapper (off epic/mutagen-wrd @ ca1c2b6)

Files touched:
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex
- test/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal_test.exs
- test/mutagen_ex/mutators_property_test.exs
- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs

Tests added:
- MutagenEx.Mutators.LiteralTest "match?/1 — __block__-wrapped values" (3 cases)
- MutagenEx.Mutators.LiteralTest "mutate/1 — __block__-wrapped values" (4 cases incl. token-strip semantics)
- MutagenEx.Mutators.LiteralTest "validate/1" — 2 new cases for wrapped values
- MutagenEx.Mutators.LiteralTest "AST round-trip (mutagen.mutators.r6)" — 2 cases (integer + boolean)
- MutagenEx.MutatorsPropertyTest "Literal (`__block__`-wrapped booleans)" involutive r5
- MutagenEx.MutatorsPropertyTest "Literal (`__block__`-wrapped 0/1)" involutive r5
- MutagenEx.MutatorsPropertyTest "Literal (`__block__`-wrapped, with `:token`)" token-strip + Macro.to_string
- MutagenEx.MutationEnumeratorTest "literal `__block__` wrapper end-to-end" (3 cases: bool site lands at wrapper line, int site lands at wrapper line, uncovered wrapper filtered before validate)

Verification stubs flipped: none new — the existing mutators.v1/v2/v3/v4 stubs were already `execute: true` and continue to pass. The new e2e enumerator test runs under the default mix test suite (no @tag :e2e_slow); the project verification command `mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test` passes (1 doctest, 322 tests, 0 failures, 13 excluded).

Deferred must requirements:
- decisions.ex literal floor (e2e Scenario 2a) → mutagen-wrd.16. The ticket's `__block__`-wrapper resolution is in place and verified end-to-end with a hand-built wrapped literal. But Elixir 1.19.5's parser does NOT wrap the literals in `test/fixtures/lane_project/lib/lane_fixture/decisions.ex` (`0` in `n > 0`, `1` in `do: 1`, etc.) — those appear as bare children of operator / clause-head tuples and carry no line metadata. The enumerator's `node_line/1 → nil → is_nil(line) → acc` filter at mutation_enumerator.ex:224 still drops them. Reaching the decisions.ex floor requires an enumerator-level parent-line-inheritance pass; filed as mutagen-wrd.16 (DISCOVERY: Literal mutator floor on decisions.ex needs enumerator parent-line-inheritance). Scenario 2a's `@tag :skip` therefore remains in place.

Summary: Added `__block__`-wrapper `match?/1`, `mutate/1`, and `validate/1` clauses to `lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex` so a literal parsed with `token_metadata: true` and surfaced as `{:__block__, meta, [value]}` is matched, mutated (preserving positional meta so the enumerator attributes the swap to the wrapper's line), and validated. `mutate/1` strips the stale `:token` metadata so `Macro.to_string/1` renders the swapped value rather than reproducing the original source token (preserves `mutagen.mutators.r6`'s AST→source round trip). End-to-end coverage in `mutation_enumerator_test.exs` "literal `__block__` wrapper end-to-end" demonstrates a hand-built wrapped literal produces a site at the wrapper's line with stable site_id and meta-preserving `mutated_ast`.

title: DISCOVERY: Literal mutator does not match parsed AST (token_metadata wraps literals)
